textDisplay: It's even more than that. A lot of these high school, middle school movies of Hollywood always depict a false reality. Yeah I know it's fiction, but everytime it's the same ole' characters but just with different names & appearances sorta. It's still the same semi-attractive or attractive protagonist & the goofy sidekick friend who are portrayed as losers & then the stereotypical dumb jock or big kid, who's sometimes attractive but is usually portrayed is being physically unattractive or mostly unattractive in intelligence. It would be nice if Hollywood could try to portray reality a little bit, but still have that entertainment value. It can still be a fun movie but not based on these cliche's we've been fed for decades. Growing up I liked Degrassi: The Next Generation before the 2010 season that butchered everything. The storylines were raw & gripping, has it's fiction but still good. Yeah there's some stereotypes in it I admit, but the characters weren't one sided or in a box, they had flaws & even the losers sometimes were bullies as well. It just would be nice to see something different, especially for kids of this generation. Anyways sorry for the really long rant lol.

textDisplay:
This.. this isnt the plot of the book...


This is nothing like the book..
The plot isnt "All the kids team up and break all the rules to get back at the mean bully principal!" No, its "One kid wants to get popular by breaking all the rules, but also has trouble at home with his step-dad."
The Principal is suprisingly a minor character, while here he's the main character. Most movie adaptions at least try to be similar to the book, but this..

This looks like its own thing, and they just got a licence for the books and slapped it on.
